description: Split monolithic parser.ts (1600+ lines) into modular files: core.ts, expressions.ts, statements.ts, types.ts, variables.ts, declarations.ts. Use ParserContext dependency injection pattern.
title: [Epic] Parser Architecture Refactoring

description:
attached_molecule: sl-wisp-xpk6
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T09:27:24Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Create isMemberVisible(modifiers, context) helper in completion.ts. Apply to getMembersFromDeclarations for external access: only PUBLIC/INTERNAL methods+properties, only VAR_INPUT/VAR_OUTPUT/VAR_IN_OUT variables. Add tests for external access hiding PRIVATE/PROTECTED members.
title: Implement isMemberVisible helper + filter external dot-access

description: Move parseTypeRef, array dims, inline enums to server/src/parser/types.ts. Move parseVarBlocks, parseVarDeclarations to server/src/parser/variables.ts. Handle interdependencies (types needed in vars). All tests must pass.
title: Extract types + variables parsing to types.ts and variables.ts

description: In handleCompletion, when inside a POU body (Ctrl+Space without dot), include the enclosing POU's methods, properties, and actions. Walk EXTENDS chain adding inherited PUBLIC/PROTECTED members. Assign proper CompletionItemKind. Add tests.
title: Add unqualified local completion for own methods/properties

description:
attached_molecule: sl-wisp-dk0f
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T09:59:10Z
dispatched_by: mayor

In handleCompletion, resolve THIS^ to current FB + base classes via EXTENDS chain. Apply isMemberVisible with 'this' context for local, 'super' for inherited. SUPER^ shows PUBLIC+PROTECTED but NOT PRIVATE. Add tests for THIS^/SUPER^ inheritance scenarios. Depends on isMemberVisible helper from sl-2fw.
title: Fix THIS^ and SUPER^ completion with inheritance + visibility

description:
attached_molecule: sl-wisp-nwvi
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-06T09:33:55Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Create server/src/twincat/typeRegistry.ts with resolveVarintType(value): string based on Phase 1 findings. Update libraryZipReader.ts readVarint to capture type index varints alongside parameter names. Apply type lookup to LibrarySymbol inputs/outputs.
notes: Phase 1 research proved types are NOT in compiled-library varints (encrypted in __languagemodel.auxiliary). Adapted approach: created typeRegistry.ts with ground-truth IEC 61131-3 type mappings for standard FBs (SR, RS, R_TRIG, F_TRIG, CTU, CTD, CTUD, TP, TON, TOF, LTP, LTON, LTOF, SEMA, LCTU, LCTD, LCTUD + string functions). Integrated resolveParamType() into libraryZipReader.ts extractCompiledSymbols(). Added 4 type assertion tests to compiledLibExtraction.test.ts. 344/361 tests pass (17 pre-existing failures from missing fixtures).
title: Implement typeRegistry.ts + integrate with libraryZipReader
Pre-existing: 12 compiledLibExtraction tests + 1 serverStartup test failing on main

title: Extract expression parsing to expressions.ts

Create tools/analyze-type-varints.js to dump raw varint streams for varied FBs (F_TRIG/BOOL, TON/TIME, CTU/WORD). Cross-reference to find varints that correlate with parameter data types. Compare schema table varints against CODESYS type enums. Document mapping. Must validate across 15+ POUs.
notes: Tool created and committed: tools/analyze-type-varints.js. Branch: polecat/quartz/sl-wp9@mmeo8w45. Commit: 52c8414. BLOCKED on git push - no git credential helper configured for https://github.com. The tool covers all requirements: varint dump for ground-truth POUs, cross-POU comparison, CODESYS type enum correlation, differential analysis, string table type search, schema table analysis. Validates across 15+ POUs (SR, RS, R_TRIG, F_TRIG, CTU, CTD, CTUD, TP, TON, TOF, LTP, LTON, LTOF, SEMA, LCTU, LCTD, LCTUD).
title: Create varint analysis tool + type correlation research

description:
Full polecat work lifecycle from assignment through completion.

This molecule guides a polecat through a complete work assignment. Each step
has clear entry/exit criteria and specific commands to run. A polecat can
crash after any step and resume from the last completed step.

## Polecat Contract (Self-Cleaning Model)

You are a self-cleaning worker. You:
1. Receive work via your hook (formula checklist + issue)
2. Work through formula steps in order (shown inline at prime time)
3. Complete and self-clean via `gt done` (submit + nuke yourself)
4. You are GONE - Refinery merges from MQ

**Self-cleaning:** When you run `gt done`, you push your work, submit to MQ,
nuke your sandbox, and exit. There is no idle state. Done means gone.

**Important:** This formula defines the workflow template. Steps are shown inline
when you run `gt prime` — there are no separate step beads to close. Work through
the checklist, then run `gt done`.

**Speed principle:** You run the full gate suite AFTER rebasing onto the target
branch (pre-verify step). This enables the refinery to fast-path merge your MR
in ~5 seconds instead of re-running gates. If pre-verification is skipped or
stale, the refinery falls through to normal gate execution.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Fix pre-existing failures on main (Refinery owns main health)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |
| base_branch | sling vars | The base branch to rebase on (default: main) |
| setup_command | rig config | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | rig config | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | rig config | Test command. Empty = skip. Rig must configure for its language. |
| lint_command | rig config | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| build_command | rig config | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Build fails | Fix it. Do not proceed if it won't compile. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |
title: mol-polecat-work

description:
Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to their effective target branches one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (quality review, if enabled)      │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).

## CRITICAL: Read Step Instructions Before Acting

Before executing ANY step, you MUST run `bd show <step-id>` and read the full
description. Steps contain **Config:** values that override default behavior.
If a config says to skip, you skip. If it specifies a command, you use that
exact command — not your own assumption. Do NOT guess what a step requires
based on the step title or your role knowledge. The step description is the
source of truth.

## Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| wisp_type | patrol | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | true | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
| integration_branch_auto_land | false | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
| run_tests | true | Whether to run tests before merging |
| setup_command | (empty) | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | (empty) | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| lint_command | (empty) | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | go test ./... | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
| build_command | (empty) | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
| target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
| delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |
| judgment_enabled | false | Enable quality review for merges (true/false) |
| review_depth | standard | Review depth: quick, standard, or deep |

## Target Resolution Rule

When instructions reference `<rebase-target>`, `<merge-target>`, or `<verification-target>`,
resolve that placeholder with this rule:

- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "true": use MR target when present, otherwise main.
- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "false": always use main.

## FORBIDDEN Actions

- FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches to the default branch via raw git commands (`git merge`, `git push`).
  Integration branches may ONLY be landed via `gt mq integration land <epic-id>`.
  This applies regardless of `auto_land` configuration. The pre-push hook enforces this.

## Step Execution Order

You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step.
title: mol-refinery-patrol

when you run `gt prime` — there are no separate step beads to close. Work through
the checklist, then run `gt done`.

**Speed principle:** You run the full gate suite AFTER rebasing onto the target
branch (pre-verify step). This enables the refinery to fast-path merge your MR
in ~5 seconds instead of re-running gates. If pre-verification is skipped or
stale, the refinery falls through to normal gate execution.

**You do NOT:**
- Push directly to main (Refinery merges from MQ)
- Close your own issue (Refinery closes after merge)
- Wait for merge (you're gone after `gt done`)
- Fix pre-existing failures on main (Refinery owns main health)

## Variables

| Variable | Source | Description |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| issue | hook_bead | The issue ID you're assigned to work on |
| base_branch | sling vars | The base branch to rebase on (default: main) |
| setup_command | rig config | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | rig config | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | rig config | Test command. Empty = skip. Rig must configure for its language. |
| lint_command | rig config | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| build_command | rig config | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |

## Failure Modes

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| Build fails | Fix it. Do not proceed if it won't compile. |
| Blocked on external | Mail Witness for help, mark yourself stuck |
| Context filling | Use gt handoff to cycle to fresh session |
| Unsure what to do | Mail Witness, don't guess |
title: mol-polecat-work

description:
Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to their effective target branches one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (quality review, if enabled)      │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).

## CRITICAL: Read Step Instructions Before Acting

Before executing ANY step, you MUST run `bd show <step-id>` and read the full
description. Steps contain **Config:** values that override default behavior.
If a config says to skip, you skip. If it specifies a command, you use that
exact command — not your own assumption. Do NOT guess what a step requires
based on the step title or your role knowledge. The step description is the
source of truth.

## Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| wisp_type | patrol | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | true | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
| integration_branch_auto_land | false | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
| run_tests | true | Whether to run tests before merging |
| setup_command | (empty) | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | (empty) | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| lint_command | (empty) | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | go test ./... | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
| build_command | (empty) | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
| target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
| delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |
| judgment_enabled | false | Enable quality review for merges (true/false) |
| review_depth | standard | Review depth: quick, standard, or deep |

## Target Resolution Rule

When instructions reference `<rebase-target>`, `<merge-target>`, or `<verification-target>`,
resolve that placeholder with this rule:

- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "true": use MR target when present, otherwise main.
- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "false": always use main.

## FORBIDDEN Actions

- FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches to the default branch via raw git commands (`git merge`, `git push`).
  Integration branches may ONLY be landed via `gt mq integration land <epic-id>`.
  This applies regardless of `auto_land` configuration. The pre-push hook enforces this.

## Step Execution Order

You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step.

title: mol-polecat-work

description:
Per-rig worker monitor patrol loop.

The Witness is the Pit Boss for your rig. You watch polecats, nudge them toward
completion, verify clean git state before kills, and escalate stuck workers.

**You do NOT do implementation work.** Your job is oversight, not coding.

## Persistent Polecat Model (gt-4ac)

Polecats persist after work completion — sandbox is preserved for reuse:

```
Polecat lifecycle: spawning → working → mr_submitted → idle (sandbox preserved)
MR lifecycle:      created → queued → processed → merged (Refinery handles)
```

Once a polecat calls gt done and submits an MR, it transitions to idle state.
The MR lifecycle continues independently in the Refinery. The polecat is NOT
nuked — its sandbox is preserved for reuse by future slings.

**CRITICAL**: Do NOT nuke polecats with pending MRs. The refinery needs the
remote branch to exist to process the merge. Nuking deletes the remote branch
and orphans the MR. See gt-6a9d.

**Key principle**: Polecat lifecycle is separate from MR lifecycle. Polecats
go idle after work, they are NOT destroyed.

## Restart-First Policy (gt-dsgp)

The witness NEVER nukes polecats automatically. When a polecat is stuck, hung,
or has a dead agent process, the witness RESTARTS the session instead of nuking.
This preserves the polecat's worktree and branch, preventing work loss.

- Dead agent process → restart session
- Hung session (no output 30+ min) → restart session
- Stuck in gt done → restart session
- Done polecat (bead closed) → leave alone (sandbox preserved)
- Polecat with pending MR → leave alone (refinery handles)

Nuking only happens via explicit `gt polecat nuke` command from a human or Mayor.

## Design Philosophy

This patrol follows Gas Town principles:
- **Discovery over tracking**: Observe reality each cycle, with minimal agent-bead state for duration tracking
- **Beads over mail**: survey-workers discovers completion state from agent bead metadata (gt-w0br); inbox-check POLECAT_DONE is fallback only
- **Persistent by default**: Clean polecats go idle, sandbox preserved for reuse (gt-4ac)
- **Cleanup wisps for merge tracking**: Created when MR is pending in refinery
- **Task tool for parallelism**: Subagents inspect polecats, not molecule arms
- **Swim lane discipline**: Only close wisps YOU created. Wisp lifecycle for non-witness wisps is the reaper Dog's job. Report orphaned foreign wisps — never close them.

## Patrol Shape (Linear)

```
inbox-check ─► process-cleanups ─► check-refinery ─► survey-workers
                                                            │
         ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         ▼
  check-timer-gates ─► check-swarm ─► patrol-cleanup ─► context-check ─► loop-or-exit
```

No dynamic arms. No fanout gates. No persistent nudge counters.
State is discovered each cycle from reality (tmux, beads, mail).
